preFix.title: Savoury cabbage strudel
preFix.subtitle: Hand-stretched strudel pastry filled with buttered cabbage and caraway
preFix.excerpt: A savoury Austrian strudel: the pastry is made from flour, oil, and warm water, rested until elastic, then stretched by hand over a floured cloth until paper-thin. The filling is braised white cabbage with caraway, seasoned with black pepper. The pastry is rolled with the filling inside and baked until shatteringly crisp.
preFix.sourceNotes: Strudel is documented in Austrian and Austro-Hungarian cookbooks from the eighteenth century, including recipes now in the public domain. The hand-stretching technique (Ziehen) and cabbage filling are both documented in public-domain Austrian culinary literature. The flavour combination of cabbage, caraway, and black pepper in strudel pastry is a traditional regional preparation.

content.text: Strudel pastry is the most demanding hand technique in this book: a simple dough of flour, oil, and warm water that must rest long enough that the gluten has fully relaxed, then be

to near-transparency. The filling is simple: white cabbage braised slowly in butter with caraway and black pepper, left to cool before it goes into the pastry. The strudel bakes fast and is best served immediately while the pastry is still crisp.

items.name: Sunflower oil
items.prepNote: 2 tbsp, plus more for the surface

content.text: Combine the flour and salt in a large bowl. Make a well in the centre, add the oil and warm water, and mix to a rough dough. Turn out and knead vigorously for 8 to 10 minutes until the dough is very smooth and elastic; it should feel like warm plasticine. Rub the surface lightly with oil, cover tightly, and rest at room temperature for at least 45 minutes.

Melt 60 g of butter in a large pan over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the cabbage, caraway, salt, and a generous amount of black pepper. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 20 to 25 minutes until the cabbage is soft and any liquid has evaporated. The filling should be dry; wet filling makes the pastry soggy. Leave to cool completely.

Preheat the oven to 200 degrees C (conventional). Cover a large table with a clean cotton cloth and flour it well. Place the rested dough in the centre and roll it into a thin rectangle. Then, working from the centre outward, slide your hands underneath the dough with closed fists and gently ease it thinner, walking around the table as you go. It should stretch to roughly 60 cm by 80 cm, thin enough to see your hands through. Trim any thick edges. Brush the entire surface with melted butter. Spread the cool cabbage filling over two-thirds of the pastry, leaving a 5 cm border along the long edge closest to you. Lift the cloth at that edge to roll the strudel up, using the cloth to help roll and keeping it tight. Ease the roll onto a lined baking tray, seam-side down. Brush all over with melted butter. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes, brushing with more butter at the halfway point, until deep golden and shatteringly crisp. Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
